p: Welcome to the ultimate hub for K-Pop AI creation. Here you will find all the community-made tutorials to master RVC, Applio, and Dahyun Bot.
If you are new here, we recommend starting with the "What's RVC" guide to understand the technology behind the covers.
Our tutorials are written by expert creators:
kyuroll, coivmn, teosarrr, lvntrq, robinn, ShadowMikuu.
The colors of this universe are Pink and Sky Blue, representing our vibrant community!
To ensure you have the most accurate and up-to-date information, we recommend reading the official documentation from AI Hub.
RVC (Retrieval-based Voice Conversion) is a complex technology, and the AI Hub team maintains the gold standard for explaining its essentials.
Official Explanations: Deep dive into VITS and voice cloning.
Up-to-date: Information is updated as the technology evolves.
Safety: Verified links to official repositories and resources.
The link above will take you to the official AI Hub documentation.

l: How to make an AI Cover with Applio Colab
p: This guide will walk you through the process of creating AI covers using Applio on Google Colab.
This is Applio collab: Open Applio Colab
Run the "Install Applio" cell.
Install Applio
After the 1st cell is done running, run the "Start Applio" cell and wait until you see a public url pop up.
Click on the public url to access the Applio gradio.
Start Applio
Once you're on the Applio gradio, click on the "Download" tab and copy the link of the voice model you want to use.
Download Tab
Paste the link onto the "Model Link" box and press on "Download Model".
Wait until the Output Information says "Model downloaded successfully".
Download Process
Go to the "Inference" tab and click "Refresh" to have access to the voice model's .pth and index file.
Inference Tab
Upload your acapella file and press on "Convert" after the acapella file is done loading.
Conversion
After the conversion is complete, you can download the output.
You can mix this output with the instrumental using any audio editing software.
Download Output

l: How to use Dahyun's bot UVR and it's RVC feature to make ai covers by lvntrq

p: Who is Dahyun : Dahyun is firstly a member from Twice ok ! Dubu dubu! But she is also a bot created by .nw, she has her own server you'll see the link below and it's actually the easiest way to inference vocals into RVC. If you're looking for another way : there's applio but in this tutorial we will focus on Dahyun.

l: Step 1: Get access to DAHYUN RVC
p: To get access to DAHYUN RVC, you can either go to KPOP UNIVERSE and go to #dahyun-rvc or join the DAHYUN RVC server!

l: Step 2: Get your audio for UVR
p: I would recommend getting your audio from YouTube and downloading it into mp3. I use https://v6.www-y2mate.com/ to download my audios! Simply just paste the video link into the box and download whatever type of format you want! For this example, I’m going to use DIVINE by Stray Kids.
Step 2 Image
You can download audio, video, and other. For this, I’m going to download mp3 320kbps. From here, you have to wait an additional 10-15 seconds to download it.
Step 2 Download

l: Step 3 : Use UVR to separate your audio
p: There is a command within DAHYUN to help you separate audios. First, you will need to insert your audio into the box. Next, you will choose your UVR model that you want to use to separate your audio. I will tell you what every UVR model does.
Step 3 Image
[MDXC] MB InstVoc Duality v1 (by unwa | Best Vocal) - This separates the vocals (vocals as in acapella) and instrumental within the song.
[MDXC] MB Inst v1e/v1 (by unwa) - This releases the instrumental, separating it into like lead instrumental and backing instrumental in a way.
[MDXC] MB Male/Female (By aufr33 | New MedleyVox) - You won’t need to use this, unless you have female and male vocals that you want to separate.
[MDXC] Mel-Karaoke (by aufr33 and ViperX) - You can use this to extract backing vocals from lead vocals, same for [MDXC] BS-DeReverb.

[MDXC] Mel-DeNoise (by aufr33) - You also won’t need to use this, we only use it when we don’t want noise in the audio, also useful if you need to clean up your dataset a bit more! Same for [VR ARCH] De-Reverb De-Echo (by FoxJoy) and [VR ARCH] De-Echo Normal (by FoxJoy).
[VR Arch] 6-HP Karaoke - This is the cleanest way to get lead vocals!

NOTES: I'm also going to explain a little about UVR aggressiveness; This varies depending on the models we use! So keep an eye on this. You can put any number in there, but I would recommend using 25 aggressiveness for MB InstVoc Duality v1/6-HP Karaoke, as it is the ideal balance. I wouldn't recommend using another number because it would sound very distorted or worse, the audio will sound very tinny (?) When we use De-Echo or De-Noise, then we can increase the aggressiveness up to 100, since it will not affect the audio Also, please make sure that your audio file is less than 10 MB, or DAHYUN will not work!
Step 3 Models

l: Step 1 : Grab the Lead Vocals you want the model to sing
p: For this, I’m going to get XLOV HARU to sing DIVINE by Stray Kids. First, I will grab the Lead Vocals that I want Hongjoong to sing. Here’s the command for using RVC with DAHYUN:
RVC Step 1
Secondly, you need to put the Lead Vocals in the box that says “Vocal”.
RVC Step 2

Now, you need to find the model link. You can head over to #ai-voices-10 to find the HARU model or you can go over to any other AI voice channels to find the model you want! In this case, I’m going to use XLOV HARU for this example. (Credits to @MinLeo_AI for the HARU model)
RVC Step 3

There are 3 other options you can use. I would recommend the default pitch, protect, and index since a male is singing a song that has male vocals in it. If you want a female to sing a song with male vocals in it, I would use the pitch +4. If you want a male to sing a song that has female vocals in it, I would use the pitch -4. Since I’m going to leave the settings default, all you have to do is press enter and wait for it to filter.
RVC Step 4

l: Step 5 : Go in DMs to download your vocals
p: Once it says this, you can head over to your DMs and click the link to download the vocals!
RVC Step 5 Image 1 RVC Step 5 Image 2
After this, you can create this into a cover and post it on YouTube! Be careful, you may or may not get a warning for copyright reasons, it varies.

l: How to use mvsep.com to have clean acapella and extract vocals by ShadowMikuu
p: Link: https://mvsep.com/en
Click on MDX-B for the separation type, and then click UVR-MDX-NET-Voc_FT (SDR: 9.64). I like using MDX-B to filter acapellas. The output encoding doesn't matter, but I mainly use flac.
Click on the Separate button and wait a while. After the instrumental and acapella have been separated, download the file that says "Vocals".

Go back to the main page of MVSEP and set the separation type to Ultimate Vocal Remover VR. Then for the model type, click on UVR-De-Echo-Normal. Put the aggressiveness to 1.0 for the best quality. Then click on the Separate button and wait a while longer.
Here
After separating the vocals, download the file that says "Instruments", as the file that says "Vocals" has the reverbed vocals.
Go back to the main page of MVSEP and change the model type to UVR-DeEcho-DeReverb. Repeat the process from Steps 3 and 4. Afterward, you should have a clean acapella.

Return to the main page of MVSEP and change the separation type to MDX-B Karaoke (lead/back vocals). Set the karaoke model type to "Extract from vocals part (SDR lead vocals: 7.94)". It doesn't matter which output encoding you use. Click on the Separate button and wait for your vocals to be extracted.
After the vocals have been extracted, you will see five files. Download only the lead and backing vocals.

Return to the main page of MVSEP and change the separation type to Medley Vox if Dahyun isn't working for you. Then set the model type to "Extract vocals first". Import the files one at a time, and start with either the lead or backing vocals. Again, the output encoding doesn't matter. Then click on separate and wait a little longer.
Download both files after they've been separated. Do the same process for the other vocals, depending on which vocals you separated first. There's no limit duration.
Have fun creating AI covers and AI voice models, even if Dahyun is down!

l: ˖ ֹ੭୧ 𝗿𝗼𝗯𝗶𝗻𝗻 ⊹ ࣪ ⑅ - How To Train a Clean Model by robinn

p: First of all, I’m not an expert in Rvc nor a programmer, so I’m just sharing what I know. These are the steps I use; they may vary from device to device and also depend on which idol you want to train the model with. With that warning out of the way, let’s continue!

THE FORMULA Basically, there’s a formula to train a clean model: No reverb / High-quality samples + Long dataset + Enough epochs = Clean model. If you follow each part of this formula, you’ll get a good model.

My secret to finding high-quality samples is looking for them on the Kpop Stems Discord server: 🔗 https://discord.gg/tXNpJfGNkv There, you can find good stems. Search carefully, since you can use lead vocals, back vocals, and even ad-libs.

IMPORTANT: Make sure the samples don’t have other voices or ad-libs mixed in. This is a common (and serious) mistake, because if you train a model with multiple voices, the AI will make mistakes, take longer to train, and the final result will be lower quality.
Another thing to watch out for is reverb, since it will make your model sound bad. To remove it, I recommend Adobe Podcast: 🔗 https://podcast.adobe.com/en/enhance

Try to collect your idol’s vocal lines from everywhere. The more minutes of dataset you have, the more realistic and better your model will sound. You can look for covers, live stages, demos, concerts, song lines, etc.

💡 Tip: If you’re not satisfied with the length of your dataset, you can duplicate it (basically upload it twice). To be honest, this is more of a superstition—there’s no solid proof it improves the model, but many people still do it.
Want a next-level model? This is the key: epochs, epochs, and more epochs. It’s the best solution. I recommend training for 350–500 epochs, depending on the length of your dataset:
If it’s long, use around 300–350.
If it’s short, use 350–500.
And that’s it! Those are the steps I follow to train my models. Again, I’m not an expert—this is just what works for me. I hope it helps you out! Byee~ ✨ .

l: How to train with RVC Disconnected
p: This tutorial is considered outdated as RVC Disconnected is now discontinued. Use for reference only.

The most important part of creating an AI model is having a clean dataset. The cleaner the audio (no background noise or distortion), the more realistic the final voice will sound. Ensure your audios are high quality and in WAV format.
Recommended Tools:
cobalt.tools: Best option (no ads).
mvsep.com: Good online alternative (create an account to skip the queue).
Useful UVR Models:
[MDXC] MB InstVoc Duality v1: Best for extracting the acapella.
[MDXC] MB Inst v1e: To extract the instrumental.
[MDXC] Mel-Karaoke: For backing vocals.
[VR Arch] 6-HP Karaoke: Cleanest way to get lead vocals.
Aggressiveness Note: I recommend using 25 for the ideal balance. For De-Echo or De-Noise, you can go up to 100. Ensure your file is under 10MB.
This is the tedious part: removing every strange detail or background noise.
Use CTRL + L to silence selected areas.
Use CTRL + K to cut empty parts.
Tip: Combine all your song snippets into one single file BEFORE extracting the acapella. Then, clean the dataset and save it as a WAV inside a ZIP file.
Open the RVC Disconnected Colab.
Upload your .zip file to the dedicated folder in your Google Drive.
Set your model name and the target sample rate.
Epochs Configuration:
10min audio + pre-train: Increase by 100 until it sounds good.
No pre-train: 200-300 epochs.
<5min audio (no pre-train): Max 250-300 epochs.
10min audio (no pre-train): 400-500 epochs.
20min audio: 500+ epochs (do not exceed 800 to avoid overtraining).
Once finished, click "Export model to drive" and "Index Training". Download the .pth and the ADDED files, ZIP them, and upload to HuggingFace.
